info.title: Critical Mention API Documentation
info.description:
## Introduction Critical Mention provides tools to search, watch video, edit, and analyze every spoken word from the world's largest global broadcast capture system. Television and radio data (including metadata, audience data, ad equivalency, and entity mark up) immediately syncs with 2,000 global broadcast sources to add 40 hours of viewable content every 60 seconds for seamless integration of the world's most persuasive medium: television. Critical Mention customers are able to constantly monitor their exposure, ensuring their message stays on-track.
System enablers and programmers can take advantage of Critical Mention's functionality by using our API, making calls from our system and integrating the data within their own. Interested? Here's an example of what you can do with our API: 1. Programmatically retrieve results which match your keyword searches from our 2,000 broadcast sources 2. Integrate our broadcast database results into your site, allowing your customers to seamlessly access Critical Mention's rich data set 3. See your data visualized by using our analytic tools and checking for patterns. Charts can be exported for local access 4. Create and set alerts to notify you of new mentions on your keyword searches 5. All this can be done programmatically by including our API calls into the architecture of your system and then displaying or using the results. ## Issuing API calls API calls can be issued in different ways. The environment from which you issue the API affects its syntax. You can issue the API calls from: 1. Your preferred programming language 2. An HTTP utility such as cURL To specify an API from the command line you must explicitly include the API's syntax along with all the desired parameters and values: 1. curl "https://staging-partner.criticalmention.com/allmedia/session" -d "username=name&password=XXXX" ## Boolean Searching Boolean queries help construct your search. Boolean queries use operands to refine your search, searching explicitly for certain items while excluding others. Some of the major operands include the words AND, OR, NOT, the plus sign +, minus sign -, tilde ~, parentheses (), double quotes " ", and the asterisk * and question mark ? wildcards: 1. Use + to include terms: +cars +chevy +1957 will find cars AND chevy AND 1957 2. Use - to exclude terms: +cars +chevy -1957 will find cars AND chevy AND NOT 1957 3. Use double quotes to find words that occur together: "Chevy trucks" 4. Add ~ at the end for optional distance between words: "Chevy trucks"~10 - will find the words Chevy and trucks within ten words of each other 5. Use ( ) to group terms: +(chevy vehicles) -(gas mileage) 6. Use the wildcard \* to find word stems: chev\* will find "chevy", "chevrolet" 7. Use the wildcard ? to search for spelling variations: cheve??? - will find "chevette" and "chevelle" ## Authenticating to the system Each partner gets their own Critical Mention client ID and password which creates and secures your access, allowing you to make requests into the Critical Mention system.
To obtain a user access token, issue a cURL request with your user ID and password as in this example: 1. curl https://staging-partner.criticalmention.com/allmedia/session -d "username=name&password=xxxx" The following is an example of an access token: 1. {... "id": "ce8685f1-5427-4148-afd1-22e985cd387e"} To use this token, place it in the authorization header in any API calls you make: 1. curl "https://staging-partner.criticalmention.com/allmedia/channel" -H "Authorization:ce8685f1-5427-4148-afd1-22e985cd387e" **Note**: It's not necessary to use your authorization token when using this API testing interface; if you are currently logged into Critical Mention, your credentials are automatically picked up from your login session.

get.summary: Returns a list of available channels
get.description: Returns a list of available channels. You can limit the results by broadcast type or by filtering out only active or inactive channels.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description:
Boolean. Turns a specific function on or off. Values are:
* false
* true

parameters.name: broadcastType
parameters.description:
The type of broadcast, which equates to the network and market. Can be one of the following:
* Canada
* Canada ( French )
* Independent / Other
* International
* National Broadcast Networks
* National Cable
* Radio Canada
* UK Radio
* United Kingdom
* US Radio

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique channel ID.

get.summary: Lists the channels and their respective markets
get.description: Lists the channels and their respective markets.

parameters.name: mediaType
parameters.description:
limits the search results to the chosen criteria. Values: 
* tv
* radio

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of clips to fetch. Default batch number is 10.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to fetch. Default page number is 1.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description:
The column to sort by. Default sort column is dateCreated. Options:
* dateCreated - Date and time a clip was created.
* lastUpdated - Date and time a clip was last updated.
* provider - Provider of the content.
* title - Title of the content.
* name - User given name for the clip.

parameters.name: ascdesc
parameters.description:
The order of the results, descending by default. Options:
* asc - ascending
* desc - descending

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Approximate name of the provider, program title, or article title. The API will try head and tail match with the given string.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Approximate name of the clip. The API will try head and tail match with the given string.

parameters.name: mediaType
parameters.description: Media type is either tv, radio, news, or twitter.

parameters.name: archive
parameters.description:
Boolean flag for requesting archived and non-archived clips. If archive is unspecified, both archived and non-archived clips are returned. Options:
* 1 - Get archived clips only
* 0 - Get non-archived clips only

get.summary: Returns the details of a clip.
get.description: Returns the details of a clip.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the Clip to fetch.

parameters.name: highlightText
parameters.description: Option to perform keyword highlighting on clip text.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the clip to update.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name you assign the clip.

parameters.name: thumbnailTimestamp
parameters.description: Custom thumbnail timestamp of a clip in yyyymmddhhMMss format.

parameters.name: startSecondMicro
parameters.description: The starting point of the clip to be updated. Uses the UNIX timestamp format. Example: 1379447855.

parameters.name: endSecondMicro
parameters.description: The starting point of the clip to be updated. Uses the UNIX timestamp format. Example: 1379447921.

parameters.name: archive
parameters.description: Archive a clip to the cloud. Set to 1 to archive.

parameters.name: highlightText
parameters.description: Option to perform keyword highlighting on clip text.

delete.description: Deletes a saved clip. This is a hard delete of a clip ID which permanently deletes the clip, and it cannot be recovered. If you delete that clip id then later try and access it in the URL as a parameter, the system will not recognize it and return an error.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the clip to delete.

get.summary: Returns a reference to the clip's thumbnail
get.description: Returns a reference to the clip's thumbnail

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the clip to fetch

400.description: Clip id is required, clip does not exist, or Online News clips do not have a thumbnail

404.description: No thumbnail found for that clip

get.summary: Returns details on all broadcast markets
get.description: Returns details on all broadcast markets.

get.summary: Returns program details based on title search.
get.description: Returns program details based on title search.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the program on which to search.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: tv, radio, or news

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Search start data-time: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:SS

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Search end data-time: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:SS

get.summary: Lists the programming schedule record for this channel
get.description: Lists the programming schedule record for this channel.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique channel identifier.

parameters.name: minTime
parameters.description:
Exclude hits before a specified time. Time is entered in this format:
* yyyyMMddHHmmss

parameters.name: maxTime
parameters.description:
Exclude hits after a specified time. Time is entered in this format:
* yyyyMMddHHmmss

parameters.name: local
parameters.description: Boolean. Set local to 1 to get schedule filtered by local source type.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
The start date and time for a search. Entered in the format:
* YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

Example:
* 2019-01-01 09:00:00

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The end date and time for a search. Entered in the format:
* YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

Example
* 2019-01-01 09:00:00

parameters.name: requiredKeywords
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. Results must match all the specified words in the query.

parameters.name: exactPhrase
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. Results must match exactly the phrase in the query.

parameters.name: anyKeywords
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. Any subject matter words you want included in the search. The query can match any of the specified keywords.

parameters.name: excludedKeywords
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. Ignore hits with any of the specified keywords.

parameters.name: booleanQuery
parameters.description: Uses a boolean query for defining the search. Enter two or more items to construct your search.

parameters.name: languageCode
parameters.description: Filter search by language, input must be comma separated ISO 639-1 (2 letter) language codes. (e.g.: en,es,zh)

parameters.name: filterSyndicated
parameters.description: Boolean. Restricts the search to syndicated programs. This removes duplicate entries from appearing as the same data is captured from different markets. Available options are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: cTV
parameters.description: Boolean. Searches on TV content. Valid values are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: tvChannels
parameters.description: List of the numeric channel IDs arranged as comma separated values (.CSV). Each channel has a unique numeric identifier. This is only useful when ctv=1.

parameters.name: tvChannelSets
parameters.description: A grouping of channels. Entered as comma separated integers.

parameters.name: tvNetworks
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. The name corresponding to a national network. Enter the text corresponding to the network name. For example, ABC, NBC, CBS, FOX.

parameters.name: tvMarkets
parameters.description:
Filter search results by markets. Takes a comma-separated list of market IDs. Examples of market output, with associated market IDs, are:`[{"id": 1, "rank": 1, "name": "New York", "state": "NY", "country": "United States"}
* {"id": 2, "rank": 2, "name": "Los Angeles", "state": "CA", "country": "United States"}]`

parameters.name: tvStates
parameters.description: Filter tv search results by market state. For example: "NY,CA"

parameters.name: tvCountry
parameters.description: Filter tv search results by market country. For example: "US, GB"

parameters.name: tvTitle
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. The descriptive title of a TV program. This is only useful when cTV=1.

parameters.name: tvGenres
parameters.description:
The specific genre to which a clip belongs.

Some genre examples: 1. News 2. Talk 3. Public Affairs 4. Business Financial 5. News Magazine 6. Educational 7. Consumer 8. Documentary 9. Entertainment 10. Event 11. Holiday Special 12. Interview 13. Politics 14. Religious 15. Sports event

parameters.name: cRadio
parameters.description: Boolean. Searches on radio content. Valid values are * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: radioChannels
parameters.description: A list of the numeric channel IDs arranged as comma separated values (.CSV). Each channel has a unique numeric identifier. This is only useful when cRadio=1.

parameters.name: radioChannelSets
parameters.description: A grouping of channels. Entered as comma separated integers.

parameters.name: radioNetworks
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. The name corresponding to a national network. Enter the text corresponding to the network name. For example, ABC, NBC, CBS, FOX.

parameters.name: radioMarkets
parameters.description:
Filter search results by markets. Takes a comma-separated list of market IDs. Examples of market output, with associated market IDs, are:`[{"id": 1, "rank": 1, "name": "New York", "state": "NY", "country": "United States"}
* {"id": 2, "rank": 2, "name": "Los Angeles", "state": "CA", "country": "United States"}]`

parameters.name: radioStates
parameters.description: Filter radio search results by market state. For example: "NY,CA"

parameters.name: radioCountry
parameters.description: Filter radio search results by market country. For example: "US, GB"

parameters.name: radioTitle
parameters.description: Alphanumeric. The descriptive title of a radio program. This is only useful when cRadio=1.

parameters.name: radioGenres
parameters.description:
The specific genre to which a clip belongs.

Some genre examples: 1. News 2. Talk 3. Public Affairs 4. Business Financial 5. News Magazine 6. Educational 7. Consumer 8. Documentary 9. Entertainment 10. Event 11. Holiday Special 12. Interview 13. Politics 14. Religious 15. Sports event

parameters.name: podcast
parameters.description: Boolean. Search on podcast content. Valid values are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: excludedSourcePodcast
parameters.description: String. Exclude specific podcasts from search results. For example, 'Anderson Cooper 360'

parameters.name: sourcePodcast
parameters.description: String. Include specific podcasts in search results. For example, 'Anderson Cooper 360'

parameters.name: podcastCategory
parameters.description:
Search podcasts by categories.

Some category examples: 1. Business 2. Investing 3. Non-Profit 4. News Commentary

parameters.name: licensedOnly
parameters.description: Boolean. Include only licensed content in search for online and print. Valid values are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: excludeDuplicateArticles
parameters.description: Boolean. Exclude duplicate articles from search for online and print. Valid values are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: youtube
parameters.description: Boolean. Search on youtube content. Valid values are: * 0 - False * 1 - True

parameters.name: titleYoutube
parameters.description: String. Include specific youtube channels in search results. For example, 'Reuters'

parameters.name: excludedTitleYoutube
parameters.description: String. Exclude specific youtube channels from search results. For example, 'Reuters'

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Integer. The maximum number of results to return. Maximum of 500 for Search.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Integer. The entry number to start with. You can use this to return your results from a specific entry number. Enter a number 0 or greater.

parameters.name: programIds
parameters.description: Integer. The unique program identifier.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: How to sort the results: asc - ascending, desc - descending.

200.description:
adValue: national publicity dollar value from SQAD
affiliate: broadcast affiliate (ex. ABC, CBS, NPR, ESPN Radio)
alias: media type (tv, radio, news, podcast, youtube, facebook, twitter)
broadcastTZ: timezone of the original broadcast location in tz database format (ex. America/New_York, America/Chicago)
broadcastType: channel type (ex. National Broadcast Network, National Cable, Independent, US Radio)
callSign: station call letters (ex. WCBS, KNBC, CNN)
categories: the category of the Youtube clip (ex. News & Politics)
ccText: closed caption text for this one minute clip
ccTextHiWords: abbreviated closed caption text for this one minute clip containing search term (ex. policy, education)
channelId: integer identifier of the broadcast station
copyright: copyright details for podcast clip
dataSourceId: integer identifier of media type (1 for TV, 5 for Radio)
duration: clip length in seconds (all clips will be 60 seconds)
endTime: program end timestamp (ex. 20200106172959)
entities: set of data points about the podcast episode: mentions, score, type, and phrase (ex. mentions: 3, score: 0.45000001788139343, type: 'Company', phrase: 'British Broadcasting Corporation')
episode: podcast episode
episode_link: link for podcast episode
genre: program genre (ex. News, Sports, Drama)
has_captions: verifies if the clip has captions (ex. true or false)
id: n/a
language: program language (ex. English)
localAdValue: local publicity dollar value from SQAD
localNumHouseholds: local audience value from Nielsen
localTime: clip airtime in the local timezone of the broadcast station
marketCountry: broadcast country (ex. United States)
marketId: integer identifier for each local market (note: this is not the Nielsen DMA rank, which can change every year)
marketLocal: broadcast coverage area of station (Local or National)
marketName: DMA name from Nielsen
marketRank: DMA rank from Nielsen (#1 - #210)
marketState: U.S. state code, where applicable
mediaPreview: simple preview player URL link for embedding in an iFrame, used for watching this one minute clip
mediaUrl: Wordplay link for editing a clip beyond a single minute (this URL should be used to iFrame CM's player in your application)
mediaUrlNoHighlight: Wordplay link without keyword highlighting. This applies only if the API search request contains keywords
mimeType: format of file, CM handles this automatically in mediaPreview and mediaURL (video/x-flv or audio/mpeg)
numHouseholds: national audience value from Nielsen
programId: unique identifier for the broadcast program from Nielsen
resultType: TV or radio media type (cTV or cRadio)
sourceType: program type (Local, Network, or Syndicated)
startTime: program start timestamp (ex. 20200106170000)
textOnly: boolean value for whether CM has the rights to share the media content from this station
thumbnailUrl: thumbnail image from the start of this one minute clip
time: clip airtime in the API user's local time zone (ex. "Jan 06, 2020 12:08 PM EST (UTC -0500") )
timestamp: clip timestamp in UTC (ex. 20200106193500)
timezone: API user's local timezone (ex. US/Eastern)
title: program title from Nielsen
uuid: unique identifier for this one minute clip

post.summary: Authenticates and creates a new Critical TV session
post.description: Authenticates and creates a new Critical TV session. To use this call you must know the partner's username and password.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description:
Alphanumeric. The username which grants you access to a system, entered as a unique string of alphabetic characters and numbers.

In this case it is your Critical Mention partner login.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description:
Alphanumeric. The security code, phrase or word used to secure data.

In this case it is your Critical Mention password.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description:
Integer. The partner identifier which indicates an agreement with the clip's owner. Also provides a way to group objects such as orders and reports. If not present on an order, it is presumed the order is anonymous and will be asked for demographics and credit card info.

Note:

To use this parameter your account must be enabled to use ClientTRAK.
